coverage_completeness.detail: All 5 functions tested both locally (20/20 pytest) and on platform E2E (5/5 COMPLETED). Local tests cover: ParseDISStream (5 tests), ExtractEntityStates (4 tests), AnalyzeScenario (3 tests), ConvertDISToJSON (2 tests), ValidateDISStream (2 tests), wrapper format (1 test), parameter coercion (3 tests). Platform E2E tests all 5 functions against live agent with real DIS binary fixtures.

assertion_quality.detail: Assertions verify structural correctness (pdu_count, entity_count, type breakdowns), value correctness (DIS version 7, exercise ID 1, entity ID filtering), geodetic plausibility bounds (lat -90..90, lon -180..180), error contract (is_valid=false for empty, violations list populated), and output format (hex encoding valid, raw_bytes present/absent based on parameter). Platform E2E verified JSON artifacts are parseable with expected field structure.

error_case_rigor.detail: Tests cover: nonexistent file (FileNotFoundError), empty file (0 PDUs, is_valid=false with violations), truncated/malformed PDU (graceful handling without crash), string-to-int parameter coercion for entity_id, string-to-bool coercion for include_raw_bytes ('true'/'false'). All error paths produce structured JSON, never raw exceptions.

platform_test_validity.detail: All 5 platform E2E jobs reached COMPLETED status via the full Istari job lifecycle: PENDING -> CLAIMED -> VALIDATING -> RUNNING -> UPLOADING -> COMPLETED. Jobs assigned to agent e0fddacb-5ff2-4e47-b5df-9b64228e7f55, completed in 45-60s each. Output artifacts uploaded and retrievable. System created with full digital thread: configurations, snapshots, tags, living README with version history.

test_input_quality.detail: Binary DIS fixtures generated programmatically using opendis library (not hand-crafted hex). scenario_basic.dis: 3 entities x 10 timesteps + 3 FirePdu + 2 DetonationPdu = 35 PDUs, 4816 bytes. scenario_combat.dis: 2 entities x 20 timesteps + 5 fire + 5 detonation = 50 PDUs, 6760 bytes. single_entity.dis: 1 entity x 10 timesteps = 10 PDUs, 1440 bytes. Also: empty.dis (0 bytes) and sample_invalid.dis (truncated at 20 bytes). Fixtures use DIS7 protocol version, proper ECEF coordinates, monotonic timestamps, and realistic entity types (country=225/US, domain=1/land).

flaky_patterns.detail: No time-dependent assertions, no network calls in unit tests, no random data. Fixtures are deterministic (same bytes every run via conftest.py). Platform E2E uses 15s polling with 300s timeout — sufficient margin. ECEF-to-geodetic assertions use range bounds, not exact floating-point equality. No sleep-based synchronization in unit tests.

fixture_adequacy.detail: Fixtures cover: multi-entity scenarios (3 entities with different force IDs), single-entity tracking, combat scenarios (fire + detonation PDUs), empty input, truncated/malformed input, parameter coercion edge cases (string '1' for int, string 'true'/'false' for bool). Each PDU type exercised: EntityStatePdu (type 1), FirePdu (type 2), DetonationPdu (type 3). Session-scoped pytest fixtures avoid regeneration overhead.

digital_thread.snapshots: baseline + pre/post per function + documented per function + final
